title: A recovered ruin, Nandi with Shiva and Parvati, Alampur, Telangana.jpg
source_url: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A recovered ruin, Nandi with Shiva and Parvati, Alampur, Telangana.jpg
license: Creative Commons Zero, Public Domain Dedication
description:
Nandi (bull) is the vehicle (vahana) and most commonly found attendant (gaṇa) of Shiva in Hindu temples. He is typically seated in adoration, always looking towards the sanctum with a Shiva linga. He may be represented as standing or ready to charge or running, when a legend or war scene is being depicted.

The above statue is damaged (Nandi's legs), and relatively small. Colossal statues of Nandi looking towards major Shiva temples are found in many parts of India, such as Kurnool, Bhanjanagar, Mysuru, Lepakshi, Gangaikonda and other places.

The dating of this ruin is unclear.
